(born  **Stjepan Lucian Poljak**; December 13, 1889 – March 9, 1955)[[1]](#cite_note-1) was an American [neuroanatomist](/wiki/Neuroanatomy "Neuroanatomy") and [neurologist](/wiki/Neurology "Neurology") considered to be one of the most prominent neuroanatomists of the 20th century.[[2]](#cite_note-Obituary-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[Klüver, Heinrich](/wiki/Heinrich_Kl%C3%BCver "Heinrich Klüver") (1955). "Dr. Stephen Polyak, 1889–1955". *Journal of Neurophysiology*. **68** (4): 675–678. **Stephen Polyak** (born  **Stjepan Lucian Poljak**; December 13, 1889 – March 9, 1955)[[1]](#cite_note-1) was an American [neuroanatomist](/wiki/Neuroanatomy "Neuroanatomy") and [neurologist](/wiki/Neurology "Neurology") considered to be one of the most prominent neuroanatomists of the 20th century.[[2]](#cite_note-Obituary-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Polyak studied the functional structure of the [organs](/wiki/Organ_(anatomy) "Organ (anatomy)") of [sight](/wiki/Sight "Sight") and [hearing](/wiki/Hearing "Hearing"), explaining the function of the [retina](/wiki/Retina "Retina") and the [cochlea](/wiki/Cochlea "Cochlea"), and [visual](/wiki/Visual_system "Visual system") and [auditory pathways and centers](/wiki/Auditory_system "Auditory system"). He also gave a new interpretation of the basic [visual processes](/wiki/Visual_processing "Visual processing").[[2]](#cite_note-Obituary-2)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Selected works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Stephen_Polyak&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Selected works")]

* *The Retina*. University of Chicago Press. 1941. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9780598445704](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9780598445704 "Special:BookSources/9780598445704"). [OCLC](/wiki/OCLC_(identifier) "OCLC (identifier)") [999459196](https://search.worldcat.org/oclc/999459196). `{{cite book}}`: ISBN / Date incompatibility ([help](/wiki/Help:CS1_errors#invalid_isbn_date "Help:CS1 errors"))
* Klüver, Heinrich, ed. (1957). *The Vertebrate Visual System*. The University of Chicago Press. [OCLC](/wiki/OCLC_(identifier) "OCLC (identifier)") [500463687](https://search.worldcat.org/oclc/500463687).
